svg.text: Goods on Country: theory of change
From community-led design to community-owned production: how our activities create measured outcomes on Country.
Remote homes face costly, short-lived goods. Floor sleeping and dirty bedding feed skin infections linked to rheumatic heart disease; FRRR (2022): 59% of remote homes have no washing machine.
↺ Community-led, and it repeats: what we learn returns to the design with every delivery.
Off-floor sleeping; a clean-bedding pathway in
Less scabies and Strep A exposure; assets
Household income retained; first local paid
Named lead on payroll; community entity
Community plastic captured and re-used; less landfill.
A working circular loop; replacement cycles lengthen.
Healthier, more self-determining remote communities: locally-owned manufacturing and a circular economy that keeps value on Country.
Status: ● tracked (live) ◐ partial / data thin ○ modelled (proxy, not measured) □ design target
The model: community leads the design. Goods supports the building. Production transfers to community ownership.
Sources: SIH Diagnostic V4, QBE Catalysing Impact, Goods MEL review, and asset register (2026-05-26). Outcomes shown are intended; see the MEL summary for what is tracked vs modelled.
